column1: What is the primary performance goal of Vim9 script compared to legacy Vim script?
column2: Vim9 script aims to increase execution speed by 10 to 100 times through command compilation.

column1: Which command must be the first statement in a file to enable Vim9 script syntax?
column2: The vim9script command.

column1: How are comments started in Vim9 script to distinguish them from strings?
column2: Comments start with the hash (#) character.

column1: Which keyword is used to declare a mutable local variable in Vim9 script?
column2: The var keyword.

column1: In Vim9 script, which keyword creates a constant where only the variable name is immutable but the value can change?
column2: The final keyword.

column1: Which keyword defines a constant where both the variable name and the value are immutable?
column2: The const keyword.

column1: By default, what is the scope of functions and variables defined at the script level in Vim9 script?
column2: They are script-local by default.

column1: Which command is used to define a compiled function in Vim9 script?
column2: The def command.

column1: What is the standard character for string concatenation in Vim9 script?
column2: The double dot (..) operator.

column1: How does indexing a string in Vim9 script differ from legacy script?
column2: Vim9 script uses character indexes instead of byte indexes.

column1: Which keyword is used to make a script-local item available for use in other scripts?
column2: The export keyword.

column1: Which command allows a script to use items exported from another Vim9 script?
column2: The import command.

column1: What syntax is used in Vim9 script to define a lambda function?
column2: The => arrow syntax, similar to JavaScript.

column1: How is a global variable explicitly accessed in Vim9 script?
column2: By using the g: prefix.

column1: In Vim9 script, what happens when a variable is declared without an explicit type or initialiser?
column2: It defaults to a type of any and is initialised to zero, false, or empty.

column1: What is the result of evaluating an empty list or dictionary in a Vim9 script condition?
column2: The condition is evaluated as falsy.

column1: Which operator is used in Vim9 script to provide a default value when an expression is falsy?
column2: The falsy operator (??).

column1: How do you cast an expression to a specific type at compile time in Vim9 script?
column2: By placing the type name in angle brackets before the expression, such as <number>myVar.

column1: What is the purpose of the import autoload command in plugin development?
column2: It delays the loading of a script until one of its exported items is actually invoked.

column1: Which command is used to define a new class in Vim9 script?
column2: The class keyword.

column1: In a Vim9 class, what is the required prefix for referring to object members within the class definition?
column2: The this. prefix.

column1: What is the standard name for a constructor method in a Vim9 class?
column2: The new() method.

column1: Which keyword makes a class member accessible from outside the class definition?
column2: The public keyword.

column1: How are protected members designated in a Vim9 class or interface?
column2: By prefixing the member name with an underscore (_).

column1: Which keyword is used to declare class-level variables or methods that do not require an object instance?
column2: The static keyword.

column1: How many other classes can a single Vim9 class extend using inheritance?
column2: A class can extend exactly one other class.

column1: Which keyword allows a class to promise it will provide the methods specified in a separate interface?
column2: The implements keyword.

column1: In inheritance, which keyword is used by a child class to call a method of its parent class?
column2: The super. prefix.

column1: What is the primary restriction on defining interfaces regarding method bodies?
column2: Interfaces can only declare method signatures without bodies or the enddef keyword.

column1: Which keyword defines an alias for a complex type specification to improve readability?
column2: The type keyword.

column1: In Vim9 script, what is the standard naming convention for class and type alias names?
column2: They must be CamelCased and start with an uppercase letter.

column1: How does Vim9 script handle numbers that start with a leading zero, such as 0123?
column2: They are treated as decimal numbers unless they start with 0o for octal.

column1: What is the effect of reloading a Vim9 script file that does not use the noclear argument?
column2: Vim deletes all existing script-local functions and variables before reloading.

column1: Which command modifier allows the execution of a legacy Vim script command within a Vim9 script?
column2: The legacy command modifier.

column1: In a Vim9 class, what is the default accessibility of an object variable if public is not specified?
column2: It is read-only from outside the class but can be written from inside.

column1: Which tool or command is used to see the instructions generated for a compiled Vim9 function?
column2: The disassemble command.

column1: In Vim9 script, what is the requirement for white space around assignment operators like '='?
column2: White space is strictly required both before and after the operator.

column1: How do Vim9 dictionary literals differ from legacy Vim literal dictionaries (#{})?
column2: Vim9 dictionaries use curly braces ({}) with literal keys by default.

column1: In Vim9 script, what must precede a range used before an Ex command, such as %s/old/new/?
column2: The range must be prefixed with a colon (:).

column1: Which command should be used instead of :lockvar for local variables in Vim9 script?
column2: The const or final keywords.

column1: In Vim9 script, how are variable-length arguments defined in a function signature?
column2: By using an ellipsis before the name, such as ...items: list<any>.

column1: What happens if a Vim9 function tries to access the legacy a: or l: scope dictionaries?
column2: These dictionaries are unavailable in Vim9 functions to reduce overhead.

column1: Which specific value is used to signify that a function should use its default value for an optional argument?
column2: The v:none value.

column1: What is the main limitation of an abstract class in Vim9?
column2: It cannot be used to create an object instance directly.

column1: Which keyword indicates that an interface is inheriting properties from another interface?
column2: The extends keyword.

column1: In Vim9 script classes, which prefix is used to make a method only accessible within the class and its subclasses?
column2: An underscore (_) prefix.

column1: What is the purpose of the specifies keyword in a Vim9 class declaration?
column2: It declares that a class defines a specific named interface.

column1: How can you create a list with a specific item type to ensure strict type checking in Vim9 script?
column2: By using angle brackets, such as var myList: list<string>.

column1: Which function should be used instead of map() if you need to change the item types of a declared list?
column2: The mapnew() function.

column1: In Vim9 script, what character replaces '#' for identifying the alternate file name?
column2: The double percent (%%) sequence.

column1: What is the requirement for a colon when using the '->' operator for a method call at the start of a line?
column2: A colon is required to distinguish it from a range or legacy shift command.

column1: Which keyword must be used to define a class that serves only as a base and cannot be instantiated?
column2: The abstract keyword before class.

column1: How are script-local variables in legacy scripts accessed within a Vim9 compiled function?
column2: They must be accessed using the s: prefix if they are not defined at compile time.

column1: What is the effect of using 'is' or 'isnot' on two strings in Vim9 script?
column2: They check identity and currently return false as strings are copied.

column1: What is the maximum number of nested loops (for and while) allowed in Vim9 script?
column2: The depth cannot exceed 10.

column1: In a Vim9 class, can a child class override an object variable from its parent class?
column2: No, object variables cannot be overridden in child classes.

column1: Which Vim9 script command manually triggers the compilation of all functions in the current script?
column2: The defcompile command.

column1: What does the 'trim' argument do in a Vim9 heredoc assignment?
column2: It removes leading indentation from the lines in the heredoc.

column1: Which special mapping key allows a command to use the script context where the mapping was defined?
column2: The <ScriptCmd> key.

column1: In Vim9 script, which command is used to repeat a search backward?
column2: The question mark (?) followed by the search text.

column1: What error occurs if a Vim9 function is called before it is defined and compilation is attempted?
column2: The compilation fails with an error that the function is not found.

column1: How can you bypass compile-time checks for a user command that is defined after a function in Vim9?
column2: By invoking the command indirectly using the execute command.

column1: In Vim9 classes, what happens if an object variable is declared with the 'const' keyword?
column2: The variable and its value become immutable after initialisation in the constructor.

column1: What is the purpose of the 'as' keyword in an import statement?
column2: It allows the imported script's items to be accessed through a custom local namespace name.

column1: In Vim9, what is the default type assigned to a variable initialised with an empty dictionary ({}) if no type is provided?
column2: The type is inferred as dict<any>.
